text: In a sunny garden filled with tall grass and dancing daisies, Pranay was busy looking for smooth stones. Suddenly, a loud zzzz-zzzz sounded right by Pranay’s ear. Pranay froze like a statue, squeezing his eyes shut and pulling his shoulders up to his ears. He was very afraid of anything that buzzed.

A tiny, fuzzy creature landed on a bright pink petal just a few inches away. It wasn't a monster; it was a bee named Viraj. He had stripes the color of sunshine and wings that shimmered like clear glass. Viraj gave his wings a little shake and looked at Pranay with big, friendly bead eyes.

"Don't be a statue, Pranay!" Viraj buzzed softly. "I’m not here to poke you. I’m far too busy being a gardener’s best friend. Would you like to see what I do?" Pranay opened one eye, then the other. Viraj didn't look scary at all; he looked like a tiny, flying teddy bear made of soft wool.

Viraj dived headfirst into a snapdragon. When he wiggled out, his fuzzy legs were covered in bright yellow dust. "This is Viraj!" the bee explained. "It’s like magic dust. I carry this Viraj from flower to flower so they can make seeds and grow more friends next year."

Pranay crawled closer, watching the yellow dust fall. "So, you're like a mailman for flowers?" Pranay asked. Viraj the bee looped-the-loop in the air. "Exactly! Without me and my sisters moving the Viraj, the garden would be very quiet and very green, with no colorful flowers to sniff."

"But I do more than just flowers," Viraj buzzed, leading Pranay toward the vegetable patch. The bee landed on a tiny white blossom on a strawberry vine. "If I don't visit these flowers with my Viraj dust, the plant won't grow any juicy red berries for you to eat. No bees means no strawberries!"

Pranay’s mouth dropped open. He loved strawberries more than anything. He looked at the vegetable patch and saw little blossoms on the tomato plants and the cucumbers. "Do you help all of these grow, too?" Pranay asked in wonder. He realized the bees were actually helping him have snacks!

"We sure do!" Viraj chirped. "And when we aren't helping the garden, we are back at our cozy home, turning flower nectar into sweet, golden honey. It’s hard work, but we love making treats for the world." He pointed his wing toward a hollow tree in the distance where the hive was hidden.

Pranay reached out a finger, very gently. Viraj landed on the tip of Pranay's nail. He was light as a feather. Pranay realized that Viraj wasn't trying to be scary; he was just a very small worker with a very big, very important job to do for the whole Earth.

"Thank you, Viraj," Pranay whispered. "I'll make sure to plant more flowers just for you." Viraj gave a happy buzz and zoomed off toward a patch of clover. Pranay stood up and waved, no longer afraid, ready to help his tiny, golden friends keep the Earth beautiful.
